reasoning: Laurent Gbagbo, who is explicitly named in the candidate list, was removed from the Ivory Coast electoral roll prior to the 2023 local elections. This was due to his 20-year conviction related to the BCEAO robbery; a 2022 presidential pardon remitted his sentence but did not grant the amnesty required to restore his civic rights. Consequently, he is currently legally ineligible to run in the 2025 presidential election. Since the resolution criteria state the question resolves as Yes if at least one named candidate "has been removed from the electoral list or otherwise become legally ineligible to run" before July 1, 2025, the conditions for a Yes resolution are effectively already met. The only path to a No resolution would be an unexpected political agreement where President Ouattara grants Gbagbo an amnesty allowing his reinstatement before the deadline, alongside no other named candidates being disqualified. Given Ouattara's incentives to keep his primary rival off the ballot for the October 2025 election, this is extremely unlikely.
